trust_boundaries.description: PBKDF2-derived 32-byte key; only lower 16 bytes used for AES-128-CTR. Key is attacker-influenced via password file.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: decryption_key[..16] used for key, decryption_key[16..] silently discarded — only 128 bits of entropy consumed from a 256-bit derivation

trust_boundaries.description: Delegation signature loaded from .sig file on disk, deserialized as BlsSignature — NOT re-validated against the delegator public key during load.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: trust_level: unverified → used as validated. If .sig file is tampered, the proxy signer operates with an invalid delegation.

trust_boundaries.description: decode_jwt extracts module_id with signature validation explicitly disabled (insecure_disable_signature_validation). A second call to validate_jwt then checks the signature. Race or call-order confusion could allow using a decoded-but-unvalidated module_id.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: Separation of decode and validate is a design-level coupling risk; callers must always call both in correct order.

trust_boundaries.description: Raw secret key bytes returned as Vec<u8> / [u8;32] with no zeroize-on-drop. Used in ProxyStore::store_proxy_bls/ecdsa for key serialization to disk.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: Key material persists in heap memory after use.

trust_boundaries.description: GENESIS_VALIDATORS_ROOT is hardcoded to [0;32]. This is intentional for builder domains but must also be correct for COMMIT_BOOST_DOMAIN usage.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: Protocol context dimension: domain computed with zero GVR — cross-chain replay possible if domain separation relies solely on fork version.

trust_boundaries.description: No per-module restriction on which consensus pubkeys a module can request signatures for. Any module with a valid JWT can sign with any consensus key.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: Authorization scope: module_id is verified but not checked against the requested pubkey's allowed signers.

trust_boundaries.description: Plaintext proxy store writes raw secret bytes to disk as JSON. Explicitly marked 'do not use in prod' but present in codebase.
trust_boundaries.dimension_notes: Production guard is documentation only, not enforced in code.

critical_paths.name: BLS signing pipeline (consensus)
critical_paths.entry: handle_request_signature → LocalSigningManager::sign_consensus → ConsensusSigner::sign → sign_commit_boost_root → compute_domain + compute_signing_root → BlsSecretKey::sign
critical_paths.risk: HIGH — domain construction and signing root are the last line of defense against cross-protocol replay

critical_paths.name: Proxy delegation creation and validation
critical_paths.risk: HIGH — delegation binds proxy key to consensus key; if domain or message encoding is wrong, delegations are forgeable

critical_paths.name: ERC-2335 proxy key load path
critical_paths.entry: ProxyStore::load_proxies (ERC2335) → load_bls_signer / load_ecdsa_signer → BlsProxySigner construction WITHOUT re-validating delegation signature

critical_paths.name: Prysm keystore decryption
critical_paths.entry: load_from_prysm_format → pbkdf2 → AES-128-CTR (lower 16 bytes of 32-byte key) → JSON parse → ConsensusSigner
critical_paths.risk: MEDIUM — key derivation and IV handling need review; only 128 bits used from 256-bit PBKDF2 output

critical_paths.name: JWT authentication flow
critical_paths.entry: jwt_auth middleware → decode_jwt (no sig check) → validate_jwt (sig check) → module_id inserted into request extensions
critical_paths.risk: MEDIUM — two-step auth with insecure decode-first is fragile; must verify no code path uses module_id between decode and validate

critical_paths.name: ECDSA proxy signing
critical_paths.risk: MEDIUM — secp256k1 signing with COMMIT_BOOST_DOMAIN; verify EIP-712 / replay protection

dimension_map_anomalies.description: BlsSignature loaded from .sig file has trust_level=unverified but is used as validated. No call to SignedProxyDelegation::validate() in ERC2335 load path.

PBKDF2 produces 32 bytes; only decryption_key[..16] fed to AES-128-CTR. This is correct (AES-128 takes 16-byte key) but discards the upper half silently — worth confirming this matches the Prysm spec and is not a truncation bug.

GENESIS_VALIDATORS_ROOT = [0;32]. compute_domain uses this for all domains. Any chain where GVR differs means COMMIT_BOOST_DOMAIN is the same across all chains that share a fork_version, enabling cross-chain replay of signed proxy delegations.

SignRequest::Consensus carries only a pubkey + object_root. The signer service verifies the JWT module_id but does not check that module_id is authorized to sign with that consensus pubkey. Any module can request a consensus signature for any key.
BlsSigner::secret() returns [u8;32] and EcdsaSigner::secret() returns Vec<u8>. Neither type implements zeroize::Zeroize or Drop with zeroize. Key bytes may linger in heap/stack after use.

threat_model_notes: Replay prevention: SignedProxyDelegation binds delegator+proxy pubkeys and uses COMMIT_BOOST_DOMAIN. However GVR=0 means the domain is identical across all Ethereum networks sharing the same fork_version byte, enabling cross-network replay of signed delegations.

Key authentication chain: consensus_signers map is populated from SignerLoader (file / validators_dir). No chain-of-trust verification at signer service startup — keys are trusted once loaded. Delegation signatures are verified at creation but NOT at load time for ERC2335.
CSPRNG: BlsSigner::new_random() → BlsSecretKey::random(); EcdsaSigner::new_random() → PrivateKeySigner::random(). Both use OS-backed RNG via rand 0.9 with os_rng feature. Acceptable.
Downgrade resistance: No protocol version negotiation in the signing API. Not applicable here.
Authorization scope: JWT identifies the module but does not restrict which keys the module can use — all consensus keys are available to any authenticated module.

verified_findings.title: ERC-2335 proxy delegation not re-validated on load
verified_findings.description: BlsProxySigner is constructed with a delegation loaded from .sig file without calling validate(). A tampered .sig file installs an invalid delegation silently.

verified_findings.title: Any authenticated module can request consensus key signatures
verified_findings.description: handle_request_signature dispatches SignRequest::Consensus without checking that the requesting module_id is authorized for the requested pubkey. module_id is available in the Extension but never passed to sign_consensus.

verified_findings.title: GVR=0 enables cross-chain replay for custom chain deployments
verified_findings.severity: Medium (custom chains only; named chains unaffected)
verified_findings.description: GENESIS_VALIDATORS_ROOT=[0;32] used in all domain computations. Named chains safe (distinct fork_versions). Two Chain::Custom deployments sharing the same fork_version produce identical COMMIT_BOOST_DOMAINs, enabling cross-chain replay.

verified_findings.title: BLS and ECDSA secret bytes not zeroized after use
verified_findings.description: secret() returns raw key bytes with no ZeroizeOnDrop. Bytes/Vec locals in store.rs not zeroized after serialization. Both signer types derive Clone without zeroize.

verified_findings.title: Prysm keystore decryption lacks MAC/checksum verification
verified_findings.description: PrysmKeystore struct omits the crypto.checksum field. AES-128-CTR decryption proceeds without any integrity check. Attacker-crafted ciphertext in the keystore file can substitute arbitrary BLS keys as consensus signers.

verified_findings.title: Prysm PBKDF2 iteration count attacker-controllable, no minimum enforced
verified_findings.description: keystore.c read from attacker-controllable JSON file with no bounds check. c=1 degrades KDF to single HMAC call; c=u32::MAX causes DoS hang.

verified_findings.title: verify_ecdsa_signature does not reject zero address (latent)
verified_findings.description: No check that recovered != Address::ZERO. Currently only used in tests; latent risk for future callers.

verified_findings.title: blstrs_plus is an untagged internal fork of blstrs
verified_findings.description: Fork at git commit with no version tag; divergence from upstream blstrs 0.7.1 unaudited. No exploitable bug found in current usage.

verified_findings.title: JWT two-step auth (decode before validate) — FALSE POSITIVE
verified_findings.description: check_jwt_auth correctly sequences decode→lookup→validate. module_id not used for any security decision before validation. Standard multi-key JWT pattern.

verified_findings.title: Prysm AES-128-CTR key truncation (32→16 bytes) — FALSE POSITIVE
verified_findings.description: AES-128-CTR uses first 16 bytes of 32-byte PBKDF2 output. This is correct per EIP-2335 spec for AES-128.

next_steps: Remediate F-02 (HIGH): Add module authorization check before consensus key signing
Remediate F-05 (HIGH): Add checksum/MAC verification to Prysm keystore load path
Remediate F-01 (MEDIUM): Call SignedProxyDelegation::validate() in ERC-2335 load path
Remediate F-03 (MEDIUM): Add genesis_validators_root to Chain::Custom for domain separation
Remediate F-04 (MEDIUM): Wrap secret() returns in Zeroizing<>; add ZeroizeOnDrop to signer types
Remediate F-06 (MEDIUM): Add minimum/maximum bounds check on Prysm PBKDF2 c parameter
Remediate F-07 (LOW): Add zero-address guard in verify_ecdsa_signature
Address DEP-01 (INFO): Tag blstrs_plus fork; track upstream security patches
